prompt: A Unified Offline Operations Portal for a regional laboratory and fulfillment business that operates entirely without internet dependency, enabling fast retrieval, controlled workflows, and auditable reporting. The system supports multiple roles including Front Desk Staff (customer registration and order creation), Lab Technicians (sample management and result entry), Dispatch Coordinators (delivery planning within defined service areas), Analysts (operational reporting), and Administrators (configuration of dictionaries, permissions, and reference ranges). The React-based web interface provides a global typo-tolerant search with local storage of the last 20 searches per user, advanced filtering (keywords, MM/DD/YYYY date constraints, status, tags, priority, price range in USD, multi-dimensional filters), sorting, and pagination. Customers and jobs are searchable by address with address book support. Dispatch workflows include offline map-based pin placement with geofence validation against configured service-area polygons and immediate feedback. Laboratory workflows support test submission lifecycle tracking (sampling → received → in testing → reported) and a report workspace that highlights abnormal values based on configurable reference ranges, with full-text searchable archived reports. Order handling includes a detailed status timeline (placement → picking → dispatch → delivery → receipt → refund) with exception queues for timeout detection (no updates within 30 minutes during picking) and out-of-stock handling with shipment splitting prompts. The backend exposes REST APIs using Go and Echo, running on a local network or single machine. PostgreSQL persists users, roles, address books, service-area polygons, orders, order events, samples, test items, reference ranges, and report versions. All state changes generate immutable audit logs capturing actor, change, and workstation timestamp. Authentication is local username/password (minimum 10 characters) with lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes; passwords are salted and hashed. Sensitive data (customer identifiers, addresses) is encrypted at rest using per-environment keys stored on the host. Search uses PostgreSQL full-text indexing with normalized filter columns. Saved filters are user-scoped and validated to prevent overly broad exports. Report editing uses optimistic concurrency with versioning; superseded reports remain readable and require reason notes for corrections. Offline geospatial validation and delivery fee computation rely on preloaded region boundaries and route tables, with deterministic fallback to straight-line distance calculations.
database: PostgreSQL (local deployment with encrypted sensitive fields)
